Items.Author: jnp95

Items.Title: API、REST API 和 RESTful API | API, REST API and RESTful API
Items.Description:
在本文中，您将介绍 API、Rest API 和 Restful API。

表中的内容

什么是 API？

应用程序接口缩写为 API，它是一种软件中介，允许两个应用程序相互通信。 让我们看一个例子来解决这个话题，如果用户想要预订火车票，Web 应用程序会显示出发时间、车站等所有信息，


当我们从开发人员的角度来看时，开发人员希望在 Web 应用程序中显示所有火车时刻表信息，然后开发人员使用 API 从存储所有火车详细信息和信息的 Web 服务器获取这些火车信息。 然后，开发人员可以使用 API 从 Web 服务器检索数据以显示在 Web 应用程序中。

使用 API 的好处

这样做，您不会从头开始开发基于 GPS 的位置跟踪应用程序。 相反，您使用的是网站或 Web 应用程序上现有应用程序的 API。 这种方法是实现所需功能的一种更高效、更具成本效益的方法。

什么是 RESTFUL API 和 REST API？

什么是 REST API？

它是一个 API，它遵循一组规则让应用程序和服务相互通信。

客户端还可以修改服务器上的项目，甚至可以通过 REST API 向服务器添加新项目。

什么是 RESTful API？

客户端可以发出四个基本的 HTTP 请求：

端点 REST API 和 RESTful API。

当用户做出响应时，API 从 Web 应用程序或 Web 服务器发送“请求”信息，它将收到“响应”。 对于 REST API，端点是通信通道的一端。

尽管 REST 和 RESTful API 的体系结构相似，但它们略有不同。 它具有客户端-服务器、无状态、可缓存、具有统一接口的层系统，而 RESTful Web 应用程序具有 REST 架构的所有功能以及独特的附加功能。

在 REST 协议是强大的，它继承了许多安全措施，这些都是内置的架构层。 在 RESTful API 中它是多层的，并且有一个传输协议，与 REST 相比，它使系统的安全性降低。

使用 Web 服务 REST API 时，URL 的工作基于请求和响应。 它对所有使用 REST API 的企业和 IT 具有高度的适应性和用户友好性。RESTful API 与 RESTLESS Web 服务相比，它过于灵活。

在本文中，我将介绍一些关于 API、REST API、RESTful API 的基础知识。如果你们觉得这篇文章有用，请与您的朋友分享。 谢谢你。



原文:

REST API and RESTful API used in the modern state of client/server architecture in software development. In this article you will be introducing to APIs, Rest API and Restful APIs.

Table of content

● What is API?

● Benefits of using APIs

● What is a RESTful API and REST API?

● Endpoints REST API and RESTful APIs.

● Difference between REST API and RESTful API.

What is API?

Application Programme Interface abbreviated as API which is a software intermediary that allows two applications to talk to each other. Let’s look into an example to go through with this topic,If user wants to book train tickets, web application shows the departure time, stations like that all the informations,


when we look in this from the developer side, developer wants to display all the train schedule informations in the web application, then developer use the APIs to get these train informations from the web server where all the train details and informations are stored. Then developers can retrieve data from the web server using the APIs to display in the web application.

Benefits of using API

APIs needed to make the connection between application functions to handle the data from predefined processes.

● Ease of integration: APIs can be embedded with any type of software application, So it can easily integrate with the applications and functions between different websites.

● Reduce software development effort: Using APIs reduce development efforts, developers can implement APIs of Google Maps to provide the exact store location to website visitors. In doing so, you are not going to develop a GPS-based location tracking application from the ground up. Instead, you are using the APIs of an existing application on your website or web application. This approach is a more efficient and cost-effective way to achieve the required functionality.

● Security: APIs provide a secure communication gateway for different app components to interact and exchange data.

What is a RESTFUL API and REST API?

What is REST API?

REpresentational State Transfer abbreviated to REST. It is an API that follows a set of rules for an application and services to communicate with each other.

REST APIs work by fielding requests for a resource and returning all relevant information about the resource, translated into a format that clients can easily interpret (this format is determined by the API receiving requests). Clients can also modify items on the server and even add new items to the server through a REST API.

What is a RESTful API?

The RESTful API is an Application Programming Interface(API) that obeys the REST architecture constraints and interacts with RESTful web services. The RESTful API also follows the principles of REST API. RESTful APIs are more scalable and have a longer lifespan. The RESTful API uses HTTP requests to access and use data. There four basic HTTP requests a client can make are:

● GET — To retrieve a resource.

● POST — To create a new resource.

● PUT — To edit or update an existing resource.

● DELETE- To delete a resource.

Endpoints REST API and RESTful APIs.

When a user makes a response APIs send a ‘request’ information from a web application or web server, it will receive a ‘response.’ The location where APIs send requests or where resources live are endpoints. With REST APIs, an endpoint is one end of a communication channel.

Difference between REST API and RESTful APIs.

Though the architecture of both REST and RESTful API is similar, they vary with a slight difference. The REST API follows all the rules of the REST Architecture. It has a client-server, stateless, cacheable, layer system with a uniform interface, whereas the RESTful web applications have all the features of the REST architecture with unique additional features .

In REST The protocol is strong, and it inherits many security measures, which are built-in architecture layers. In RESTful APIs It is multi-layer and has a transport protocol which makes the system less secure when compared with REST.

When using web services REST API working of the URL is based on request and response. The working of RESTful is completely based on REST applications.

It is highly adaptable and user-friendly to all business enterprises and IT using REST APIs.RESTful APIs It is too flexible when compared to RESTLESS web services.

In this article I’m go through some basics about APIs, REST API, RESTful API.If you guys find this article useful , share with your friends. Thank you.
Items.Author: kavinduchethani

Items.Title: YugabyteDB谓词下推 | YugabyteDB Predicate Push Down
Items.Description:
我在之前的博客文章中写过一些非常基本的下推。 更多即将到来。 在最新的预览版中，可以启用一些下推过滤器。 这是一个简单的例子。

过滤器删除的行

我有下表，在n上没有索引：

 yugabyte =# create table demo ( id bigint primary key , value int ); CREATE TABLE yugabyte =# insert into demo ( id , value ) select n , n from generate_series ( 1 , 1000 ) n ; SELECT 1000 yugabyte =# show yb_enable_expression_pushdown ; yb_enable_expression_pushdown ------------------------------- off ( 1 row ) yugabyte =# explain ( costs off , analyze , verbose ) select * from demo where value = 1 ; QUERY PLAN ------------------------------------------------------------------- Seq Scan on public . demo ( actual time = 6 . 040 .. 6 . 058 rows = 1 loops = 1 ) Output : id , value Filter : ( demo . value = 1 ) Rows Removed by Filter : 999 Planning Time : 0 . 038 ms Execution Time : 6 . 095 ms ( 6 rows )

在yb_enable_expression_pushdown设置为off的情况下，版本2.13.2中的默认值已从远程节点读取 1000 行，由我连接的节点过滤 999 行，最终输出 1 行。

这使用 PostgreSQL 解释计划，其中操作中的rows显示最终输出， Rows Removed by Filter计算已读取的内容，然后过滤。 然后丢弃行的比例很高，这是访问效率低下的标志。 这可能需要为Filter:中的条件提供更好的索引。

结果在分布式 SQL 数据库中更糟，因为这些行除了被读取之外，还被发送到会话后端进行过滤。 这需要时间，并且增加了云环境中的网络成本。 解决方案可能是更好的索引，但数据库也可以通过将过滤器下推到远程节点来改进这一点。

远程过滤器

通过将yb_enable_expression_pushdown设置为on ，这些行在平板服务器上被过滤：

 yugabyte =# set yb_enable_expression_pushdown to on ; SET yugabyte =# explain ( costs off , analyze , verbose ) select * from demo where value = 1 ; QUERY PLAN ------------------------------------------------------------------- Seq Scan on public . demo ( actual time = 2 . 697 .. 2 . 698 rows = 1 loops = 1 ) Output : id , value Remote Filter : ( demo . value = 1 ) Planning Time : 0 . 034 ms Execution Time : 2 . 734 ms ( 5 rows )

这里的时间差异很小，因为我在同一台机器上的所有节点的实验室上运行它，但更少的行发送也意味着更少的 CPU、更少的上下文切换和更好的负载分布。 在多个可用区甚至区域的实际部署中，差异将是巨大的。

仅用于序列扫描

下推目前仅适用于 Seq Scan，它总是有意义的。 如果我创建一个“坏”索引并用提示强制它：

 yugabyte =# create unique index demoi on demo ( id ) include ( value ); CREATE INDEX yugabyte =# set yb_enable_expression_pushdown = on ; SET yugabyte =# explain ( costs off , analyze , verbose ) /*+ IndexOnlyScan(demo) */ select * from demo where value = 1 ; QUERY PLAN -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Index Only Scan using demoi on public . demo ( actual time = 6 . 825 .. 6 . 872 rows = 1 loops = 1 ) Output : id , value Filter : ( demo . value = 1 ) Rows Removed by Filter : 999 Heap Fetches : 0 Planning Time : 0 . 086 ms Execution Time : 6 . 897 ms ( 7 rows )

该索引不过滤行（没有Index Cond. ）并且所有 100 行都已返回到支持在那里过滤的 PostgreSQL。

如果您从索引访问中看到Rows Removed by Filter ，那么最好考虑一下索引。 对于此查询，索引应该on demo(value) include (id) ，或者可能是where value=1的部分索引。

我提到过，谓词下推是针对 SeqScan 实现的，但是如果您对 YugabyteDB 内部结构以及它如何重用 PostgreSQL 感兴趣，您会发现上面显示的 SeqScan 实际上是伪装的 ForeignScan。 您看到的“远程过滤器”下推位于explain.c ForeignScan中。 对于常见用法，您不需要了解实现细节。 但是很高兴知道您是否使用查询计划器提示。 例如，使用/*+ SeqScan(demo) */尝试上面的示例不会下推谓词，因为它会强制执行真正的 SeqScan 而不是隐藏的 ForeignScan。

也用于更新和删除

请注意，谓词下推也发生在 DML 上。 您可以在 SeqScan 节点中看到它。 例如，这不会删除任何行，但必须将它们全部读取，因为我没有找到它们的索引：

 yugabyte =# explain ( costs off , analyze ) delete from demo where value = 0 ; QUERY PLAN ------------------------------------------------------------------ Delete on demo ( actual time = 2 . 188 .. 2 . 188 rows = 0 loops = 1 ) -> Seq Scan on demo ( actual time = 2 . 187 .. 2 . 187 rows = 0 loops = 1 ) Remote Filter : ( value = 0 ) Planning Time : 0 . 044 ms Execution Time : 2 . 228 ms ( 5 rows )

如果没有下推，这将显示Filter: (value = 0)和Rows Removed by Filter: 999 。 这里不必将 ros 返回给 SQL 处理层。

设置 yb_enable_expression_pushdown = on

您可能想知道为什么默认情况下不启用这个很棒的功能？ 请记住，您可以使用 YugabyteDB 进行在线滚动升级。 您不想连接到较新版本的 tserver，它将过滤器推送到没有它的先前版本。 这就是原因，但是一旦你升级了整个集群，你就可以打开它。 与许多领域一样，这也将很快改进以使其自动化（滚动升级的保守默认设置，但在整个集群拥有新功能后立即启用新功能）。

哪些谓词？

当然，并不是所有的谓词都可以被下推，并且给出一个列表很快就会过时，因为你可以期待更多的实现。

开源软件的美妙之处在于测试也是开放的。 然后，如果您想了解可以下推的谓词，为什么不在回归测试中使用 grep for Remote Filter ，如下所示：

 curl - s https : // raw . githubusercontent . com / yugabyte / yugabyte - db / master / src / postgres / src / test / regress / expected / yb_select_pushdown . out \ | grep "Remote Filter" Remote Filter : ( i1 = 1 ) Remote Filter : ( t1 ~~ 'val%' :: text ) Remote Filter : ( ts1 < 'Thu Nov 11 11:11:12 2021' :: timestamp without time zone ) Remote Filter : ( ts2 < 'Thu Nov 11 11:11:10 2021 UTC' :: timestamp with time zone ) Remote Filter : isfinite ( ts1 ) Remote Filter : ( "left" ( t1 , i1 ) = 'v' :: text ) Remote Filter : (( i1 ):: double precision < ( '10' :: double precision + ( random () * '90' :: double precision ))) Remote Filter : ( ts1 IS NULL ) Remote Filter : ( ts1 IS NOT NULL ) Remote Filter : (( i1 = 1 ) OR (( NOT isfinite ( ts1 )) AND ( ts2 > 'Mon Jan 01 08:01:01 2001 UTC' :: timestamp with time zone ))) Remote Filter : CASE WHEN (( i1 % 2 ) = 0 ) THEN ( ts1 < 'Fri Nov 12 00:00:00 2021' :: timestamp without time zone ) WHEN (( i1 % 2 ) = 1 ) THEN ( ts2 > 'Sat Jan 01 07:00:00 2022 UTC' :: timestamp with time zone ) ELSE NULL :: boolean END Remote Filter : ( i1 = 1 ) Remote Filter : ( i1 = 1 ) Remote Filter : ( tag = 'foo' :: text ) Remote Filter : ( v = 'foo' :: text )

您会看到相等、不等式、空测试、布尔表达式、CASE WHEN THEN END 以及一些可以安全下推的函数。

对于我的 Oracle DBA 追随者和一些 google 搜索关键字，您可以看到这些下推与带有谓词卸载的 Oracle Exadata 智能扫描非常相似。 即使 Exadata 是一个单一的数据库机器，并且读取完整的块而不是行和列，它也有相同的目标：避免通过网络发送稍后将被过滤掉的行。 我写的是预览版 2.13.2，我建议对其进行测试并提供反馈🙏



原文:

I've written, in a previous blog post, about some very basic pushdowns. More are coming. YugabyteDB started by plugging PostgreSQL on top of the DocDB Distributed Storage and Transaction layer, and then optimize it by pushing down the relevant operations to reduce the remote calls between the nodes. In the latest preview version, some pushdown filters can be enabled. Here is a simple example.

Rows Removed by Filter

I have the following table with no index on n:

yugabyte=# create table demo (id bigint primary key, value int);
CREATE TABLE yugabyte=# insert into demo (id, value) select n, n from generate_series(1,1000) n;
SELECT 1000 yugabyte=# show yb_enable_expression_pushdown; yb_enable_expression_pushdown
------------------------------- off
(1 row) yugabyte=# explain (costs off, analyze, verbose) select * from demo where value=1; QUERY PLAN
------------------------------------------------------------------- Seq Scan on public.demo (actual time=6.040..6.058 rows=1 loops=1) Output: id, value Filter: (demo.value = 1) Rows Removed by Filter: 999 Planning Time: 0.038 ms Execution Time: 6.095 ms
(6 rows)

With yb_enable_expression_pushdown set to off, the default in version 2.13.2, 1000 rows have been read from the remote nodes, 999 filtered by the node I'm connected to, to finally output 1 row.

This uses the PostgreSQL explain plan where the rows in the operation shows the final output and the Rows Removed by Filter counts what has been read, and filtered afterwards. Then the ratio of discarded rows is high, it is a sign of inefficient access. This probably requires a better index for the condition in Filter:.

The consequence is worse in a distributed SQL database because those rows, in addition to being read, are sent to the session backend to be filtered there. This takes time, and increases the network cost in a cloud environment. The solution may be in better indexing, but the database can also improve this by pushing down the filter to the remote node.

Remote Filter

By setting yb_enable_expression_pushdown to on, the rows are filtered on the tablet servers:

yugabyte=# set yb_enable_expression_pushdown to on;
SET yugabyte=# explain (costs off, analyze, verbose) select * from demo where value=1; QUERY PLAN
------------------------------------------------------------------- Seq Scan on public.demo (actual time=2.697..2.698 rows=1 loops=1) Output: id, value Remote Filter: (demo.value = 1) Planning Time: 0.034 ms Execution Time: 2.734 ms
(5 rows)

The difference in time is small here, as I'm running this on a lab with all nodes on the same machine, but less rows to send also means less CPU, less context switches, and a better distribution of the load. In a real deployment over multiple availability zone, or even regions, the difference will be huge.

Only for Seq Scan

The pushdowns are currently implemented for Seq Scan only, where it always makes sense. If I create a "bad" index and force it with hint:

yugabyte=# create unique index demoi on demo(id) include (value);
CREATE INDEX yugabyte=# set yb_enable_expression_pushdown=on;
SET yugabyte=# explain (costs off, analyze, verbose) /*+ IndexOnlyScan(demo) */ select * from demo where value=1; QUERY PLAN
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Index Only Scan using demoi on public.demo (actual time=6.825..6.872 rows=1 loops=1) Output: id, value Filter: (demo.value = 1) Rows Removed by Filter: 999 Heap Fetches: 0 Planning Time: 0.086 ms Execution Time: 6.897 ms
(7 rows)

This index doesn't filter the rows (no Index Cond.) and all 100 rows have been returned to the PostgreSQL backed to be filtered there.

If you see Rows Removed by Filter from an index access, then better think about the index. For this query, the index should be on demo(value) include (id) or maybe a partial index where value=1.

I mentioned that predicate pushdown is implemented for SeqScan, but if you are interested in YugabyteDB internals, and how it reuses PostgreSQL, you will see that the SeqScan displayed above is actually a ForeignScan in disguise. The Foreign Data Wrapper is the PostgreSQL operation that has the code to manage remote access, which makes sense in a distributed database. The pushdown you see as "Remote Filter" is in explain.c ForeignScan. And a ForeignScan for a SELECT on a YugabyteDB table is actually displayed as "Seq Scan" in explain.c to avoid confusion for PostgreSQL users. You don't need this knowledge of implementation details for common usage. But it is good to know if you use query planner hints. For example, trying the above example with a /*+ SeqScan(demo) */ will not push down the predicate because is forces the true SeqScan rather than the hidden ForeignScan.

Also for Updates and Delete

Note that predicate push down also occur on DML. You see it in the SeqScan node. For example, this removes no rows but has to read them all as I have no index to find them:

yugabyte=# explain (costs off, analyze) delete from demo where value=0; QUERY PLAN
------------------------------------------------------------------ Delete on demo (actual time=2.188..2.188 rows=0 loops=1) -> Seq Scan on demo (actual time=2.187..2.187 rows=0 loops=1) Remote Filter: (value = 0) Planning Time: 0.044 ms Execution Time: 2.228 ms
(5 rows)

Without pushdown, this would have displayed Filter: (value = 0) and Rows Removed by Filter: 999. Here no ros had to be returned to the SQL processing layer.

set yb_enable_expression_pushdown = on

You may wonder why this awesome feature is not enabled by default? Remember that you can do online rolling upgrades with YugabyteDB. You don't want to be connected to a newer version tserver that will push down a filter to a previous version that doesn't have it. That's the reason, but as soon as you have upgraded the whole cluster, you can switch it on. Like in many areas, this will also improve soon to make this automated (conservative defaults for rolling upgrades but enabling new feature as soon as the whole cluster has them).

Which predicates?

Of course not all predicates can be pushed down and giving a list would be obsolete soon as you can expect more to be implemented.

The beauty of open source software is that the tests are also open. Then, if you want to get an idea of the predicates that can be pushed down, why not grep for Remote Filter on the regression tests, like this:

curl -s https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yugabyte/yugabyte-db/master/src/postgres/src/test/regress/expected/yb_select_pushdown.out \
| grep "Remote Filter" Remote Filter: (i1 = 1) Remote Filter: (t1 ~~ 'val%'::text) Remote Filter: (ts1 < 'Thu Nov 11 11:11:12 2021'::timestamp without time zone) Remote Filter: (ts2 < 'Thu Nov 11 11:11:10 2021 UTC'::timestamp with time zone) Remote Filter: isfinite(ts1) Remote Filter: ("left"(t1, i1) = 'v'::text) Remote Filter: ((i1)::double precision < ('10'::double precision + (random() * '90'::double precision))) Remote Filter: (ts1 IS NULL) Remote Filter: (ts1 IS NOT NULL) Remote Filter: ((i1 = 1) OR ((NOT isfinite(ts1)) AND (ts2 > 'Mon Jan 01 08:01:01 2001 UTC'::timestamp with time zone))) Remote Filter: CASE WHEN ((i1 % 2) = 0) THEN (ts1 < 'Fri Nov 12 00:00:00 2021'::timestamp without time zone) WHEN ((i1 % 2) = 1) THEN (ts2 > 'Sat Jan 01 07:00:00 2022 UTC'::timestamp with time zone) ELSE NULL::boolean END Remote Filter: (i1 = 1) Remote Filter: (i1 = 1) Remote Filter: (tag = 'foo'::text) Remote Filter: (v = 'foo'::text)

You see equality, inequality, null test, boolean expressions, CASE WHEN THEN END, and some functions that are safe to push down.

And for my Oracle DBA followers, and some google search keywords, you can see these pushdowns are very similar to Oracle Exadata Smart Scan with predicate offloading. Even if Exadata is a monolithic database machine, and reads full blocks rather than rows and columns, it has the same goal: avoid sending rows though the network that will be filtered out later. The grep "Remote Filter" is my equivalent of select * from V$SQLFN_METADATA where offloadable='YES' 😋

I'm writing this as of preview version 2.13.2 and I recommend to to test it and give feedback 🙏

Items.Title: 对 LLVM 上的高性能编程感兴趣？ 有一个新社区适合你。 | Interested in high performance programming on the LLVM? There's a new community for you.
Items.Description:
我们最近的 Forem 亮点的共同主题是 DEV 可以作为一个真正有效的市政厅——我们一直在寻求将信号与噪音分开，并使 DEV 成为所有开发商的高质量目的地。 虽然 DEV 提供了广度，但我们从未打算让它提供专家经常需要的每个主题的深度。

这就是为什么我很高兴与大家分享一个新的 Forem，它致力于为 Julia 的主题提供必要的深度：一种通用但高度动态的编程语言。 认识Julia 语言社区。

如果您有兴趣跟上这个非常迷人的编码环境，我强烈建议您查看这个空间。

欢迎来到 Julia Forem！ 如果您正在阅读本文，那么您正在使用我们的新 Forem 实例。 你可能是...

我们很高兴在forem.julialang.org上分享新的#JuliaLang @forem 🎉

今天就加入并开始写作✍️

如果您对 Julia 感到好奇，我建议您立即潜入。

最后一件事。 您会注意到“使用 Forem 注册”作为这些 Forem 的一个选项。 这是我们现在提供的一项服务，可以最无缝地导航生态系统并管理您的身份。 我们希望您能够在无需携带全部数据的情况下充分发挥自我，因此从长远来看，使这个分布式生态系统尽可能简单是非常重要的。 创建一个Forem 帐户并通过您的设置将其与您的 DEV 帐户连接。 🙂 🙂



原文:

The common theme across our recent Forem highlights is that DEV can serve as a really effective town hall — and we are always seeking to separate signal from noise and make DEV a high-quality destination for all developers. While DEV delivers breadth, we never intended for it to provide the depth in every subject that is so often needed for specialists.

That's why I'm excited to share that there's a new Forem on the block that's dedicated to providing that necessary depth for the subject of Julia: a general-purpose but highly-dynamic programming language. Meet the Julia Language Community.

If you are interested in keeping up with this really fascinating coding environment, I highly recommend checking this space out.

Welcome to the Julia Forem! If you are reading this, you are on our new Forem instance. You might be...

We are excited to share that the new #JuliaLang @forem is live at forem.julialang.org 🎉

The Julia forem is the best place to write, discuss, and share Julia content! Jump in today and start writing ✍️

forem.julialang.org is administered by the official Julia core team members, so the ultimate goal is all about the needs of the Julia community and language itself. If you are curious about Julia, I recommend diving in right away.

One last thing. You’ll notice "Sign up with Forem" as an option on these Forems. This is a service we now offer to most seamlessly navigate the ecosystem and manage your identity. We want you to be able to bring your full self without having to bring your full data, so making this distributed ecosystem as straightforward as possible in the long run is really important. Create a Forem account and connect it with your DEV account via your settings. 🙂
Items.Author: ben

Items.Title: 如何在 WordPress 中修复延迟解析 JavaScript | How To Fix Defer Parsing Of JavaScript In WordPress
Items.Description:
为什么要推迟 JavaScript 的解析？

当您打开一个网站时，您的浏览器将从服务器接收该网站的内容并从上到下加载代码。 但是，如果它找到 JavaScript，加载过程将被中断，直到它完成所有 JavaScript 的下载。

要解决此问题，您可以延迟 JavaScript 的解析，这允许浏览器加载完整内容而无需等待脚本加载。 通过执行此任务，JavaScript 解析不会对您网站的加载时间产生负面影响。

如何推迟 WordPress 中 JavaScript 的解析？

本节将向您展示延迟解析 JavaScript 的分步指南。 最重要的是，我们还将向您展示如何分析问题并在应用任务后测试更改。

分析网站

要确定是否应该在 WordPress 网站中延迟解析 JavaScript，请使用 GTMetrix 等网站速度测试工具对其进行分析。 例如，这里是一个网站在实施该方法之前的性能结果。
它表明存在关于 JavaScript 解析的问题，这意味着网站需要推迟它。

在没有插件的情况下延迟解析 JavaScript（仅适用于 WordPress 用户）

为此，您需要登录到您的 wp-admin。 然后单击外观，然后单击主题编辑器。 在里面找到 Function.php 文件并粘贴以下代码

function defer_parsing_of_js ( $url ) { if ( FALSE === strpos( $url, '.js' ) ) return $url; if ( strpos( $url, 'jquery.js' ) ) return $url; return “$url' defer “; }

参考博客
如何在 WordPress 中修复延迟解析 JavaScript



原文:

Why Should You Defer Parsing of JavaScript?

When you open a website, your browser will receive the site’s content from the server and loads the code from top to bottom. However, if it finds JavaScript, the loading process will be interrupted until it finishes downloading all the JavaScript.

To solve this issue, you can defer parsing of JavaScript, which allows the browser to load the full content without waiting for the scripts to load. By implementing this task, JavaScript parsing won’t negatively affect your website’s loading time.

How to Defer Parsing of JavaScript in WordPress?

This section will show you a step-by-step guide to defer parsing of JavaScript. To top it off, we’ll also show you how to analyze the problem and test the changes after applying the task.

Analyze the site

To find out whether you should defer parsing of JavaScript in your WordPress website, analyze it using site speed testing tools like GTMetrix. As an example, here is the performance result of a website before implementing the method.
It shows that there’s an issue regarding JavaScript parsing, which means the website site needs to defer it.

Defer Parsing of JavaScript Without plugin (Only For WordPress users)

For that, you need to log in to your wp-admin. Then click over appearance then theme editor. Inside that find the Function.php file and paste the following code

function defer_parsing_of_js ( $url ) { if ( FALSE === strpos( $url, ‘.js’ ) ) return $url; if ( strpos( $url, ‘jquery.js’ ) ) return $url; return “$url’ defer “; }

add_filter( ‘clean_url’, ‘defer_parsing_of_js’, 11, 1 );

Reference Blog
How To Fix Defer Parsing Of JavaScript In WordPress
Items.Author: jibahaw144

Items.Title: 最常见的 HTTP 标头 | Most Common HTTP Headers
Items.Description:
网络抓取世界中一个常见且重复的问题是如何避免被目标服务器阻止？ 以及，如何提高检索数据的质量？

今天，让我们看看增加流畅数据收集机会的有用方法之一——使用 HTTP 标头。

用于 Web 抓取的 HTTP 标头

当然，有经过验证的资源和技术，例如使用代理或练习轮换 IP 地址，这将帮助您的网络爬虫避免阻塞。

然而，另一种有时被忽视的技术是使用和优化 HTTP 标头。 这种做法将大大降低您的网络爬虫被各种数据源阻塞的机会，并确保检索到的数据是高质量的。

如果您对 HTTP 标头知之甚少，请不要惊慌，因为我们在官方博客上介绍了HTTP 标头是什么，并讨论了它们在网络抓取过程中是如何连接的。

在本文中，我们将揭示需要使用和优化的 5 个最常见的 HTTP 标头，并为您提供背后的原因。

以下是最常见的 HTTP 标头的简要列表：

标题示例值
文本/html
用户代理版本/12.1.1 Safari/605.1.15

验证 User-Agent 请求标头是 Web 服务器的常见做法，它是允许数据源识别可疑请求的第一个检查。 例如，当网络抓取正在进行时，大量请求正在传送到网络服务器，如果 User-Agent 请求标头相同，它看起来就像是一个类似机器人的活动。 因此，经验丰富的网络抓取投注者将操纵和区分 User-Agent 标头字符串，从而允许描绘多个有机用户的会话。

因此，当涉及到 User-Agent 请求标头时，请记住经常更改此标头携带的信息，这将大大降低您被阻止的几率。

我们需要提到的一件事是，当 Web 服务器无法识别首选语言（例如通过 URL）时，这个特定的标头通常会发挥作用。

也就是说，Accept-Language 请求标头的关键是相关性。 必须确保设置的语言与数据目标域和客户端的 IP 位置一致。 仅仅是因为，如果来自同一个客户端的请求以多种语言出现，这会引起 Web 服务器对类似机器人行为（非有机请求方法）的怀疑，因此，它们可能会阻止 Web 抓取过程。

换句话说，它表明所需的信息在从 Web 服务器发送到客户端时可以被压缩（如果 Web 服务器可以处理）。

接受编码但是，经过优化后，它可以节省流量，从流量负载的角度来看，这对您和 Web 服务器都是双赢的。 您仍然可以获得所需的信息（只是压缩的），并且 Web 服务器不会通过传输大量流量来浪费其资源。

接受

测试/html,应用程序/xhtml+xml,应用程序/x
听起来很简单，但是网络抓取的一个常见问题是忽略或忘记根据网络服务器接受的格式配置请求标头。 如果 Accept 请求标头配置得当，它将导致客户端和服务器之间的通信更加有机，从而降低网络爬虫被阻塞的机会。

推荐人

在阻止抓取过程时，Referer 请求标头似乎几乎没有影响，但实际上确实如此。 想想一个随机的自然用户的互联网使用模式。 该用户很可能会在强大的互联网上冲浪并忘记一天的时间。 因此，如果您想将网络爬虫的流量描绘得更自然，只需在开始网络爬虫会话之前指定一个随机网站。

关键是不要急于求成，而是采取这个相当简单的步骤。 因此，请记住始终设置Referer请求标头，并增加您在Web服务器实施的反抓取措施下滑倒的机会。

把它包起来

既然我们已经提供了常见的 HTTP 请求标头列表，您就知道要配置哪些 Web 抓取标头，并且通过这样做，您可以增加您的 Web 抓取器成功且高效地进行数据提取操作的机会。

可以肯定地说，您对网络抓取的技术方面了解得越多，您的网络抓取结果就会越富有成效。 明智地使用这些知识，你的网络爬虫将更有效和高效地工作，这是给定的！



原文:

A common and repetitive question in the world of web scraping is how to avoid getting blocked by target servers? And, how to increase the quality of retrieved data?

Today, let’s look at one of the useful methods of increasing your chances for smooth data collection – using HTTP headers.

HTTP headers for web scraping

Of course, there are proven resources and techniques, such as the use of a proxy or practicing rotating IP addresses that will help your web scraper to avoid blocks.

However, another sometimes overlooked technique is to use and optimize HTTP headers. This practice will significantly decrease your web scraper’s chances of getting blocked by various data sources, and also ensure that the retrieved data is of high quality.

Don’t be alarmed if you have little knowledge about HTTP headers, as we covered what HTTP headers are and discussed how they are connected in the web scraping process on our official blog.

In this article, we are revealing the 5 most common HTTP headers that need to be used and optimized, and provide you with the reasoning behind it.

Here is the brief list of the most common HTTP headers:

Header Example value
HTTP header User-Agent Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:12.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/12.0
HTTP header Accept-Language en-US
HTTP header Accept-Encoding gzip, deflate
HTTP headers Accept text/html
HTTP header Referer http://www.google.com/

HTTP headers enable both the client and server to transfer further details within the request or response.

1. HTTP header User-Agent

The User-Agent request header passes information related to the identification of application type, operating system, software, and its version, and allows for data target to decide what type of HTML layout to use in response i.e. mobile, tablet, or pc.

User-Agent Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_14_5)AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko)

Version/12.1.1 Safari/605.1.15

Authenticating the User-Agent request header is a common practice by web servers, and it is the first check that allows data sources to identify suspicious requests. For instance, when web scraping is in process, numerous requests are traveling to the web server, and if User-Agent request headers are identical, it will seem as if it is a bot-like activity. Hence, experienced web scraping punters will manipulate and differentiate User-Agent header strings, which consequently allow portraying multiple organic users’ sessions.

So, when it comes to the User-Agent request header, remember to frequently alter the information this header carries, which will allow you to substantially reduce your odds of getting blocked.

2. HTTP header Accept-Language

The Accept-Language request header passes information indicating to a web server which languages the client understands, and which particular language is preferred when the web server sends the response back.

One thing we need to mention is that this particular header usually comes into play when web servers are unable to identify the preferred language e.g. via URL.

That said, the key with the Accept-Language request header is relevance. It is essential to ensure that set languages are in accordance with the data-target domain and client’s IP location. Simply because, if requests from the same client would appear in multiple languages this would raise suspicions to the web server of bot-like behavior (non-organic request approach), and consequently, they might block the web scraping process.

3. HTTP header Accept-Encoding

The Accept-Encoding request header notifies the web server of what compression algorithm to use when the request is handled. In other words, it states that the required information can be compressed (if the web server can handle it) when being sent out from the web server to the client.

Accept-Encoding br, gzip, deflate

However, when optimized it allows saving traffic volume, which is a win-win situation for both you and the web server from the traffic load perspective. You still get the required information (just compressed), and the web server isn’t wasting its resources by transferring a huge load of traffic.

4. HTTP header Accept

The Accept request header falls into a content negotiation category, and its purpose is to notify the web server on what type of data format can be returned to the client.

Accept test/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/x
ml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8

It’s as simple as it sounds, but a common hiccup with web scraping is overlooking or forgetting to configure the request header accordingly to the web server’s accepted format. If the Accept request header is configured suitably, it will result in more organic communication between the client and the server, and consequently, decrease the web scraper’s chances of getting blocked.

5. HTTP header Referer

The Referer request header provides the previous web page’s address before the request is sent to the web server.

Referer http://www.google.com/

It might seem that the Referer request header has very little impact when it comes to blocking the scraping process, when in fact, it actually does. Think of a random organic user’s internet usage patterns. This user is quite likely surfing the mighty internet and losing track of hours in a day. Hence, if you want to portray the web scraper’s traffic to seem more organic, simply specify a random website before starting a web scraping session.

The key is not to jump the gun and instead take this rather straightforward step. Hence, remember to always set up the Referer request header, and boost your chances of slipping under anti-scraping measures implemented by web servers.

Wrapping it up

Now that we have provided the list of common HTTP request headers, you know which web scraping headers to configure, and by doing so, you can increase your web scraper’s chances of a successful and efficient data extraction operation.

It’s safe to state that the more you know about the technical side of web scraping, the more fruitful your web scraping results will be. Use this knowledge wisely, and it’s a given that your web scraper will work more effectively and efficiently!

Items.Title: 软件测试以及如何将其用作技术入门工作 | Software Testing and How You Can Use it as a Tech Entry Job
Items.Description:
软件测试是一项技术工作，并没有得到应有的重视。 在积极开发客户使用的产品时，让客户满意至关重要； 如果每个版本都破坏了软件中的其他内容，那么没有人会对新功能感到满意。

测试人员在软件开发中发挥着重要作用，您可以通过以下两种方式之一来考虑它：

  • 作为自己的职业——特别是如果你可以非常关注细节并且不介意重复性任务
  • 作为技术领域的入门工作，您将能够利用它来实现您的长期职业目标

在我的职业生涯中，我见过很少有人使用软件测试作为扩展工作档案的坚实基础。

入门级：手动测试

在最肤浅的层面上，测试是以与用户相同的方式使用应用程序并检查它是否以预期的方式运行。 成功完成它所需的技能如下：

  • 作为用户，您需要了解应用程序。 您将必须知道如何理解应用程序的域以及如何使用界面。 例如，如果您从事物流软件的工作，您将需要了解该行业的一些基本概念和术语。
  • 分析技能——报告错误的方式有很大的不同。 写作——对于你发现的每一个错误，你都需要将它传达给开发团队。 您的交流最有可能通过文本（信息、电子邮件或票据）进行。 如果您想学习一些可以使您更好地进行书面交流的东西，请查看这篇文章。

好消息是所有这些技能对于任何 IT 角色都是必需的。

验收测试

验收测试是人们通常手动完成的事情之一。 它不一定是一个单独的角色——我见过或听说过：

  • 代理机构使用他们的客户作为测试人员——所以如果客户接受了申请，那么这意味着它是正确的
  • 产品公司非常快速地部署更改并监控用户行为模式的突然变化

这些是一些选项，但通常有一个专门的测试人员在关闭所有工单之前检查所有工单并仔细检查它们是否确实得到解决。 在一个团队中，您可以期望一个测试人员为几个开发人员服务。

回归测试

除了检查要更改的内容之外，还有必要监控无意的更改——回归。 每个积极维护的代码库都会收到无数的更改：

  • 框架或第三方库的更新
  • 代码重用可以创建应用程序不同部分的耦合——因此对一个用例的修复可能会导致另一个用例出现问题

为了确保这些更改不会破坏应用程序，最好的方法是检查应用程序的所有现有功能并确保它仍然按预期工作。 这是很多工作。 当必须手动完成时，您很可能只将精力集中在应用程序必须支持的最关键操作上。

如果进行更系统的努力，将会有记录下来的测试场景供您遵循。 通过这种方式，团队确保了每个测试会话都是相同的——无论谁执行它。

自动化测试

自动化测试是形式化测试场景的又一个进步——在这里您将场景编写为可执行代码。 在 Web 环境中，您经常听到它们被称为端到端 (E2E) 测试，因为目标是测试从前端到后端的所有内容。

测试自动化让您受益于计算机的优势：

  • 它们提供更快的执行时间
  • 他们永远不会感到无聊
  • 他们非常精确

作为交换，您的工作从点击应用程序到维护一套自动化测试。

在我的职业生涯中，我主要从事前端 JavaScript，但我也做了很多测试自动化。 它有时具有挑战性，例如：

  • 最初的设置可能很耗时——尤其是当您之前没有经验时
  • 低质量的测试会随机失败——给出假阴性结果并破坏对测试的信任
  • 编写 E2E 常常让人感觉像是一件苦差事

另一方面，自动化可以加快质量保证，并将成本降低到每次提交都可以通过完整验证的程度。 如果您有兴趣将 E2E 添加到您的项目中，可以查看这篇文章。

作为测试人员你可以学到什么

尽管有些人喜欢测试并将其变成职业，但这份工作为您提供了无数机会来培养在其他道路上也有用的技能。

测试为您提供了了解公司的绝佳机会。 如果您以前没有在科技公司工作过，那么您将有可能踏入大门并获得内幕消息。 例如，您将能够在实践中学习以下内容：

过程

创建软件与其说是科学，不如说是艺术，但成功的团队在他们的工作流程中有着许多相似之处。 了解它的最佳方法是自己参与该过程。 许多公司正在使用某种形式的敏捷方法。 例如，就我而言，过去 10 年我在使用 Scrum 方法的团队中度过。

一个人可以在工作中学习它，但我发现在面试中存在关于熟悉它的问题。 不知道它不应该成为破坏交易的因素，但得知有些人加入了之前的 Scrum 经验总是让人放心。

工装

对于来自完全不同行业的人来说，熟悉公司使用的工具可能很有价值。 为了给你背景，我每天使用的工具：

  • 您可以期望每家公司都使用提供相似功能的不同工具。 学习它们都没有挑战性，但了解它们可以节省您的时间。

    在代码层面上，仅了解现实世界中开发团队的框架就可以帮助您选择合适的学习框架。 你会在网上看到很多流行语驱动的建议——新的和闪亮的东西得到了不成比例的更多关注，而行业的工作骡子却被忽视了。 知道您所在地区至少有一家公司在生产中使用框架 X 可能强烈表明学习此框架对您来说是一项很好的投资。 这些知识特别有用，因为您可以轻松地检查他们是否在该技术方面有空缺，以及他们找到具有该技能的人有多难。

    指导机会

    一家好的公司应该对支持你的职业发展感兴趣。 如果幸运的话，您将有可能在公司内部建立导师关系，例如：

    • 如果管理层支持您想要发展的方向，您可以尝试让官方导师帮助您解决出现的问题，或者
    • 一位愿意指导您完成学习过程的友好同事，或
    • 代码审查，您的同事就您的更改向您提供反馈——您总是可以加倍努力来处理反馈并了解其背后的原因

    进度选项

    假设您想将软件测试用作不同技术部分的蹦床，您有哪些选择？

    编程

    如果您的长期计划是成为一名程序员，您可以利用测试作为锻炼肌肉的机会。 测试自动化就是编程：唯一的区别是你编写的是测试用例，而不是特性——你学习的是测试框架而不是应用程序框架。

    因为自动化 E2E 测试是代码并且通常在每次提交时运行，所以您应该能够从团队中的开发人员那里获得支持，以创建高质量的测试。 如果有一个代码审查过程，那么通过它进行测试也是完全有意义的。 这些对您来说可能是非常宝贵的经验，并且可以加快您的学习速度。

    产品开发角色

    这些角色代表了您可以选择的另一个方向。 当您进行验收测试时，您将获得客户要求并检查程序是否满足它。 您很自然地处于客户和开发团队之间。 还有其他角色坐在类似的地方：

    • 产品管理
    • 产品设计
    • 用户体验设计

    对于这些类型的工作，您需要深入了解客户，并学习如何解决他们所面临的贵公司正在构建的技术所面临的问题。 作为测试人员，您将经常参与定义需求的循环——尤其是在没有其他人从事产品设计工作的情况下。 如果您培养了这些角色所需的技能，您应该能够利用这些机会朝这个方向发展。

    我认识一些走这条路的人，这看起来很自然——职责只是越来越多地转向找出需求，而不是检查需求。 最后，他们将大部分时间都花在了产品上而不是测试上。

    另一项非显而易见的工作是成为 Scrum 方法的内部专家，并帮助您的同事尽可能提高工作效率。 这个角色是 Scrum 方法的一部分。 不是每家公司都使用它，但 Scrum 足够流行，足以成为一个有效的职业选择。 这很可能不是一个全职职位。 在一个只有少数人的团队中，您不会有足够的问题或障碍让您忙碌——但它仍然可以成为您技能和日常职责的有趣补充。

    供应问题

    没有多少人愿意将测试作为工作。 问题是：

    • 技术人员看不起它，宁愿构建应用程序而不是测试它们，并且
    • 非技术人员不知道他们或根本不考虑在那里申请。

    我不能保证在测试中找到一份工作会很容易——我只从事过编程工作——但我相信这可能是一个值得考虑的有趣选择。 与许多其他 IT 角色相比，它的进入门槛应该更低。

    想要阅读更多内容？

    如果您发现本文有帮助，您可以在此处继续阅读有关自动化测试的信息。

原文:

Software testing is a tech job that doesn’t get as much attention as it deserves. It’s critical to keep customers happy while the product they are using is actively developed; nobody would be pleased with new features if each release breaks something else in the software.

Testers play an important role in software development, and you can consider it in one of two ways:

  • as a career of its own—especially if you can give a lot of attention to detail and don’t mind repetitive tasks
  • as an entry job in tech that you will be able to leverage to achieve your long-term career goals

Over the course of my career, I’ve seen few people using software testing as a solid base to expand their work profile.

Entry level: manual testing

At the most superficial level, testing is using the application the same way the users do and checking whether it behaves in an expected way. The skills you need to do it successfully are as follows:

  • You need to know the application as a user. You will have to know how to understand the application’s domain and how to use the interface. For example, if you work on logistic software, you will need knowledge of some basic concepts and terminology of that industry.
  • Analytical skills—how you report a bug makes a lot of difference. “Something is off” is more difficult to follow than “as a user X, I do A, then B, then cancel C midway, and on the screen 1, we get value M when I expected T.”
  • Writing—for every bug you find, you will need to communicate it to the development team. Your communication will be performed most likely with text—messages, emails or tickets. If you want to learn a few things that will make you better at communicating in writing, have a look at this article.

The good thing is that all those skills are necessary for any IT role.

Acceptance testing

Acceptance testing is one of the things that people usually do manually. It’s not necessarily a separate role—I’ve seen or heard about:

  • agencies using their client as a tester—so if the customer accepts the application, then it means it’s correct
  • product companies deploying changes very fast and monitoring for sudden changes in user behavior patterns

Those are some options, but it often makes sense to have a dedicated tester go through all tickets before they are closed and double check that they are indeed resolved. In a team, you can expect one tester for a few developers.

Regression testing

Besides checking what’s meant to be changed, it’s necessary to monitor for unintentional changes—regressions. Every codebase that is actively maintained receives countless changes:

  • an update to framework or third-party-libraries
  • code reuse can create coupling of different parts of an application—so a fix for one use case can cause issues in another

To make sure that those changes are not breaking the application, the best approach is to check all the existing features of the application and ensure that it still works as expected. That’s a lot of work. When it has to be done manually, you’ll most likely just focus your effort on the most critical operation that the application has to support.

In case of more systematic efforts, there will be testing scenarios written down for you to follow. In this way, the team has ensured that each testing session is the same—regardless of who executes it.

Automated testing

Automated testing is another step up from formalized testing scenarios—here you are writing the scenarios as executable code. In the context of the web, you often hear them called End-to-End (E2E) tests because the goal is to test everything from frontend to backend.

Test automatization allows you to benefit from the strengths of computers:

  • they provide faster execution time
  • they never get bored
  • they are very precise

In exchange, your job goes from clicking through the application to maintaining a suite of automated tests.

In my career, I mostly worked on frontend JavaScript, but I did a good deal of test automation as well. It’s sometimes challenging, as:

  • the original setup can be time-consuming—especially when you have no previous experience
  • low-quality tests will fail randomly—giving false-negative results and undermining trust in the testing
  • writing E2E often feels like a chore

On the other hand, automation allows for speeding up quality assurance, and it lowers the cost to the point where every commit can go through a complete verification. If you are interested in adding E2E to your project, you can check out this article.

What you can learn as a tester

Whereas some people enjoy testing and making it into a career, this job gives you countless opportunities to build skills that will be useful in other paths as well.

Testing gives you a great opportunity to learn about the company. If you weren’t working in tech companies before, you would have a possibility to get your foot in the door and access insider knowledge. For example, you will be able to learn the following things in practice:

Process

Creating software is more art than science, but successful teams share numerous similarities in their workflow. The best way of learning about it is to participate in that process yourself. Many companies are using some form of Agile methodologies. For example, in my case, I spent the past 10 years in teams that use Scrum methodology.

Scrum is relatively rigid—it defines plenty of meetings, roles, and expectations. One can learn it on a job, but I find that there are questions about familiarity with it during interviews. Not knowing it shouldn’t be a dealbreaker, but it’s always reassuring to learn that some join with some previous experience in Scrum.

Tooling

For someone coming from an entirely different industry, getting familiar with the tools used in the company can be valuable. To give you the context, the tools that I use every day:

  • Slack—team messaging application
  • Jira—bug tracking software
  • GitLab—git hosting provider
  • Google Meet—online meetings

You can expect every company to use different tools providing similar functionalities. None of them are challenging to learn, but knowing them can save you time.

On a code level, just knowing the frameworks of dev teams in the real world can help you choose the right one to learn. You’ll see a lot of buzzword-driven advice online—where new and shiny things are getting disproportionately more attention while work mules of the industry are overlooked. Knowing that at least one company in your area is using framework X in production can be a strong indication that learning this framework is a good investment for you. This knowledge is especially useful as you can easily check if they have openings in that technology and how difficult it is for them to find people with that skill set.

Mentoring opportunity

A good company should be interested in supporting your professional growth. If you are lucky, you’ll have the possibility to establish a mentoring relationship inside the company, for example:

  • if management supports the direction in which you want to develop, you can try getting an official mentor to help you with questions as they appear, or
  • a friendly colleague who is willing to guide you through your learning process, or
  • code review where your colleagues give you feedback on your changes—you can always go the extra mile to process the feedback and learn the reasoning behind it

Progress options

Assuming you want to use software testing as a trampoline to different parts of tech, what are your options?

Programming

If your long-term plan is to become a programmer, you can use testing as an opportunity to build your muscles. Test automatization is programming: the only difference is that you program the test cases, not features—and that you learn test frameworks and not application frameworks.

Because automated E2E tests are code and are often run on every commit, you should be able to find support from developers on your team to create good quality tests. If there is a code review process in place, then putting tests through it makes complete sense too. Those can be very valuable experiences for you, and it can speed up your learning.

Product development roles

These roles represent another direction that you could choose. As you do acceptance testing, you are given the client requirements and check whether the program is fulfilling it. You are naturally placed between the client and the dev team. There are other roles that sit in similar places:

  • product management
  • product designed
  • UX design

For those kinds of jobs, you will need to get to know customers well, and learn how to resolve issues they are facing with the technology your company is building. As a tester, you will be frequently in the loop as the requirements are defined—especially if there is nobody else whose job is product design. If you develop skills needed for those roles, you should be able to leverage those opportunities to move in this direction.

I know a few people who went this path, and it looked very natural—the duties simply shifted more and more toward figuring out requirements and less on checking them. Finally, they were spending most of their time working on the product instead of testing it.

Scrum master

Another non-obvious job is becoming an internal expert on Scrum methodology and helping out your colleagues to be as productive as possible. This role is part of Scrum methodology. Not every company uses it, but Scrum is popular enough for this to be a valid career option. It’s most likely not a full-time position. In a team with a handful of people, you will not have enough issues or blockers to keep you busy—but it can still be an interesting addition to your skill set and daily duties.

Supply issue

Not many people are willing to do testing as a job. The problem is:

  • technical people look down on it and would rather build applications and not test them, and
  • non-technical people are not aware of them or simply don’t consider applying there.

I cannot guarantee that landing a job in testing will be easy—I have only worked in programming—but I’m sure it can be an interesting option to consider. It should have a lower barrier to entry than many other IT roles.

[dev.to]

Want to read more?

If you found this article helpful, you can continue reading about automated testing here.

Items.Title: 在 WordPress 中禁用 WooCommerce 评论选项卡的方法 | ways to Disable WooCommerce Reviews Tab In WordPress
Items.Description:
当使用像 WooCommerce 这样的插件时，很难避免定制，很多。 在我最近的项目中，我采用 WooCommerce 来实现没有购物车经验的产品管理。 你猜对了，禁用和隐藏这么多选项。

选项 1：使用插件

禁用评论是一个插件，允许管理员全局禁用任何帖子类型的评论，包括自定义帖子类型。 由于评论只是使用产品的评论功能（自定义帖子类型）。 所以我们可以通过禁用对产品的评论来禁用产品评论并隐藏产品页面中的选项卡。

选项 2：woocommerce_product_tabs 过滤器

另一种方法是使用 WooCommerce 插件提供的 woocommerce_product_tabs 过滤器。 过滤器让我们可以直接控制隐藏不需要的选项卡。

 // Disable product review (tab) function woo_remove_product_tabs($tabs) { unset($tabs['description']); // Remove Description tab unset($tabs['additional_information']); // Remove Additional Information tab unset($tabs['reviews']); // Remove Reviews tab return $tabs; } add_filter( 'woocommerce_product_tabs', 'woo_remove_product_tabs', 98 );

参考博客
在 WordPress 中禁用 WooCommerce 评论选项卡的方法



原文:

When working with plugin like WooCommerce, it is hard to avoid customization, a lots of it. In my recent project, I adopted WooCommerce to achieve product management without shopping cart experience. You guessed it, disable and hide so many options.

Option 1: Using a Plugin

Disable Comments is a plugin that allows administrators to globally disable comments on any post type, including custom post type. As review is just using comments feature for Product (custom post type). So we can disable product review and hide the tab in product page by just disabling comment on Product.

Option 2: woocommerce_product_tabs filter

The other way is to use woocommerce_product_tabs filter provided by WooCommerce plugin. The filter gives us direct control to hide the tab(s) we don’t need.

// Disable product review (tab)
function woo_remove_product_tabs($tabs) {
    unset($tabs['description']);                // Remove Description tab
    unset($tabs['additional_information']);     // Remove Additional Information tab
    unset($tabs['reviews']);                    // Remove Reviews tab

    return $tabs;
}

add_filter( 'woocommerce_product_tabs', 'woo_remove_product_tabs', 98 );

Reference Blog
ways to Disable WooCommerce Reviews Tab In WordPress

Items.Title: 认识 Webpixels 2.0 | Meet Webpixels 2.0
Items.Description:
经过 4 个月的不断开发和改进，新的 Webpixels 网站终于上线了。 此更新的重点是组件库的工作方式和整体体验。 目标是让浏览示例以及预览和复制您需要的内容变得更加容易。

以下是您可以从今天开始尝试的一些新内容：

访问新的 Webpixels 网站

新组件浏览器

现在您可以在一个地方查看所有类别并过滤掉您需要的类别，而不是拥有类别并单击其中的每一个。 此外，还有一个搜索栏可以快速获得您想要的结果。

浏览组件

在新的性感模态中预览组件

找到所需的组件后，单击它以预览 UI 和代码。 现在一切都发生在一个地方，因此我们希望这将使整个体验变得更好，减少点击次数和花费时间从图书馆中获取您需要的东西。

实时代码编辑

找到您需要的组件，然后单击它以预览 UI 以及代码。 在最新版本中，您将能够在将其复制到您的项目之前对其进行调整并查看更改。

将组件组织到集合中

由于我们的组件是不断增长的库的一部分，因此集合将允许您根据类别或项目类型创建更小批量的元素。

列出模板的新选项卡

与其将组件和模板混合在一个地方，我们认为将它们分成两个单独的部分会更好。 通过这种方式，您可以更接近原子原则。

改进的文档

我们还有很多东西要添加到我们的文档中，但到目前为止，我们对结果非常满意。 我们将继续添加更多示例和功能，因为 Bootstrap 通过 v5.2 更新开始了它的重大举措。

以下是您在不久的将来应该期待的一些事情：

  • 组件 CSS 变量
  • 新的助手和实用程序
  • 根、组件和实用程序的变量组
  • 改进的主题功能
  • 开箱即用的暗模式支持

我们预计将在一个月后发布@webpixels/css 的下一个主要更新。

谢谢🙏

我们正式推出新的 Webpixels 网站已经一年了。 构建人们认为有用并为他们的项目带来价值的东西是一种很棒的感觉。

现在我们已经为下一个里程碑做好了准备。

希望你喜欢它。



原文:


The new Webpixels website is finally live, after 4 months of continuous development and improvements. This update is focused on how the component library works and the overall experience. The goal is to make it much easier to navigate through examples and preview and copy the stuff you need.

Here are some of the new stuff you can try starting today:

Visit the new Webpixels website

New component explorer

Instead of having categories and clicking each one of them, now you’ll be able to see them all in one place and filter out the ones you need. Also, there is a search bar to get the result you want quickly.

Browse components

Preview components in a new sexy modal

Once you find the component you need, click on it to preview the UI, but also the code. Everything happens now in one place, so we hope it will make the whole experience a much better one, with fewer clicks and time spent to get the things you need out of the library.

Live code editing

Find the component you need, and click on it to preview the UI, but also the code. In the latest version, you will be able to tweak it and see the changes before copying it into your project.

Organize components into collections

Since our components are part of an ever-growing library, collections will allow you to create smaller batches of elements based on the category or the project’s type.

New tab for listing templates

Instead of mixing components and templates in one place, we thought it would be much better to separate them in two separate sections. This way you can work closer to Atomic Principles.

**See all Templates**

Improved documentation

We still have many things to add to our docs, but we are pretty happy with the result, so far. We'll keep adding more examples and features, now that Bootstrap started its big move with the v5.2 update.

Here are some things you should expect in the near future:

  • Component CSS variables
  • New helpers and utilities
  • Groups of variables for root, components, and utilities
  • Improved theming capabilities
  • Dark mode support out of the box

We expect to release the next major update for @webpixels/css one month from now.

Thank you 🙏

It’s been one year since we officially launched the new Webpixels website. It is such a great feeling to build something that people find useful and brings value to their projects.

And now we are ready for the next milestone.

Hope you enjoyed it.
Items.Author: extrabright

Items.Title: 我们可以在无服务器应用程序中进行更好的故障检测 | We can do better failure detection in serverless applications
Items.Description:
传统上，在白盒监控中，错误报告是通过第三方库实现的，这些库可以捕获故障并将其传达给外部服务，并在出现问题时通知开发人员。 我在这里争论的是，对于托管服务，这可以通过更少的努力、没有代理并且没有性能开销来实现。

事实上，您不应该在 AWS Lambda 中使用经典的错误报告工具有很多原因。 其中最关键的是代码中的错误处理库对 Lambda 特定的故障视而不见，例如超时、错误配置的包和内存不足故障。 此外，还有一个覆盖率问题——为每个函数实现错误报告需要大量工作。 每当您向基础架构添加服务时，您都必须为其设置错误跟踪和监控，而忘记这样做可能会导致系统出现盲点。

幸运的是，这些问题可以很容易地解决，并且在大多数情况下，只需采用新的工具和开发实践即可。

关于“可观察性”这个词

在深入了解细节之前，了解可观察性背后的想法很重要。 这并不意味着您将拥有可见性，或者您甚至可以立即监控您的服务。 这意味着系统通过输出数据使自己易于理解，使开发人员能够就系统的当前或过去状态提出任何类型的任意问题。 幸运的是，信息发送方面在 AWS 中得到了很好的实现，例如，无服务器用户有机会获得可见性，而无需在他们的代码中专门实现额外的东西。

除了 CloudWatch 日志，我们还可以利用 AWS API 来发现资源，利用 X-ray 和 CloudTrail 来跟踪和连接执行流程。

我们今天可以更好地进行故障检测

检测所有功能的故障并将它们与特定调用连接、查看日志并为它们提取 X 射线跟踪的能力显着减少了解决故障情况的平均时间。

让我们分解一下

通常，基于日志的错误检测和可见性的唯一先决条件是将日志推送到 CloudWatch（在大多数情况下这是默认设置）。 从那里开始，我们可以做一些智能模式匹配和推断来检测失败场景。

最重要的是，日志包含许多其他数据，这些数据表明延迟和内存使用情况，并允许我们使用 AWS X-ray 连接请求并搜索特定请求的跟踪报告。 所有这些使我们能够收集大量上下文，以便了解在特定情况下出了什么问题。

以下是您搜索特定 Lambda 请求时 X 射线跟踪所包含的内容。 这使您能够捕获 Lambda 函数所涉及的服务中的错误。

结论

随着托管和分布式服务的出现，监控环境将不得不经历重大变化以跟上现代云应用程序的步伐。 目前，devops 开销是希望在生产中使用无服务器并将其用于关键任务应用程序的公司的最大障碍之一。 我们在Dashbird的团队希望一次解决一个问题。

在这里注册试试看。



原文:

Traditionally in white-box monitoring, error reporting has been achieved with third party libraries, that catch and communicate failures to external services and notify developers whenever a problem occurrs. I'm here to argue that for managed services this can be achieved with less effort, no agents and without performance overhead.

In fact, there's a lot of reasons why you shouldn't use classical error-reporting tools in AWS Lambda. Most critical of them is that error-handling libraries in the code are blind to Lambda specific failures, such as timeouts, wrongly configured packages and out of memory failures. In addition, there is an issue with coverage -- implementing error reporting for each function is a lot of work. Whenever you add a service to your infrastructure, you must go through setting up error tracking and monitoring for it and forgotting to do it can result in blind-spots in your system.

Luckily, those problems can be solved quite easily and in most cases it's just a matter of adopting new tooling and development practices.

About the word "observability"

Before getting into details it's important to understand the idea behind observability. It doesn't mean that you'll have visibility or that you can even monitor your service right of the bat. It means that the system makes itself understandable by outputting data that enables the developer to ask any kind of arbitrary questions about the current or past state of the system. Fortunately the information emitting aspect is well implemented in AWS and serverless users for example have an opportunity to get visibility without specifically implementing extra stuff in their code.

Apart from CloudWatch logs, we could leverage AWS APIs for resource discovery and X-ray and CloudTrail for tracing and connecting execution flows.

We can make failure detection better today

The ability to detect failures across all functions and connect them with specific invocations, view logs and pull X-ray traces for them significally reduces the mean time to resolution in failure scenarios.

Lets break it down

The only prerequisite for log-based error detection and visibility in general is that logs are pushed to CloudWatch (in most cases that is the default). From there on, we can do some smart pattern matching and deduction to detect failure scenarios.

On top of that, logs contain a lot of other data that indicate latency and memory usage and allow us to connect requests with AWS X-ray and search for a trace report for a specific request. All this allows us to gather a lot of context in order to understand what went wrong in a particular case.

Here's what an X-ray trace contains when you search for it for a specific Lambda request. This enables you to catch errors in services your Lambda function touches.

Conclusion

With the emergence of managed and distributed services, the monitoring landscape will have to go through a significant change to keep up with modern cloud applications. Currently, devops overhead is one of the biggest obstacles for companies looking to use serverless in production and rely on it for mission-critical applications. Our team at Dashbird is hoping to solve that one problem at a time.

Dashbird takes about 5 minutes to set up, after which you will get full visibility into your serverless applications. Give it a try by signing up here.

Items.Title: 专家提供准确的文凭翻译服务 | Accurate Diploma Translation Services By Experts
Items.Description:
专业文凭翻译服务

认证文凭翻译服务对于移民美国的人来说是必不可少的。 这是对文凭翻译的详细见解。

认证文凭翻译服务

申请绿卡或移民美国工作、教育或其他目的的人需要翻译他们的外语文件，包括高中文凭翻译、出生证明、文凭等的翻译。 政府当局需要精确的翻译来阅读和理解文件中包含的所有内容。

专业文凭翻译服务

文凭是由监管机构签署的官方文件，表明两人之间的婚姻有效。 它是从外国移民到美国或在美国在外国正式结婚时需要翻译的最重要的文件之一。

作为重要的证据文件，当局为接收所需信息制定了一定的标准。 这就是为什么政府当局需要经过认证的翻译。

认证文凭翻译服务是指翻译文件随附的附加文件，证明翻译人员能够进行准确和完整的翻译。 必须聘请专业认证的翻译人员，他们是源语言和目标语言的语言专家，并且在认证翻译方面经验丰富，以进行准确和高质量的翻译。 拥有专家团队的知名翻译机构大多受雇于寻求认证文凭翻译的人。

美国公民及移民服务局是负责外国人合法移民美国的联邦机构。 它需要所有非英语文件的认证翻译，包括文凭、出生证明、护照等。

美国公民及移民服务局的认证文凭翻译是指根据翻译人员的能力验证翻译文件的准确性和完整性。 它还必须包含翻译者的姓名、地址、签名、日期和认证时间。 由公司翻译的，翻译文件必须加盖公司印章和印章，以证明其准确性和完整性。

有时，文件可能需要公证。 它需要公证人见证认证文件的签署，同时验证翻译者的身份。

为避免在向 USCIS 提交翻译文件时出错，建议聘请专业翻译公司的服务，以保证由源语言和目标语言的母语语言专家提供满意的服务，并在翻译重要文件方面具有丰富的经验。文凭之类的移民文件。

外包文凭翻译的好处

通常情况下，人们会求助于先进的软件翻译工具或双语候选人为美国公民及移民服务局提供在线文凭翻译。 好吧，由于美国对他们收到的数据很严格，不恰当的翻译和认证翻译真实性的任何不确定性都可能导致美国公民及移民服务局暂停这一过程。 未提供进一步证明的，驳回申请。 因此，建议聘请专业且经验丰富的文凭翻译服务，以保证准确性和完整性。 美国公民及移民服务局很少拒绝此类文件。

重要的是要了解 USCIS 在美国是一个非常重要的机构。 移民文件的翻译必须遵守适当的规定。 软件工具和双语候选人无疑可以逐字翻译文档中的内容，但无法保持原始文档的上下文完整性和格式。

因此，聘请专业服务是成功的关键。



原文:

Professional Diploma Translation Services

Certified Diploma translation services are essential for people migrating into the US. Here's a detailed insight into Diploma translations.

Certified Diploma Translation Services

People applying for a green card or migrating into the US for work, education, or other purposes require translation of their foreign language documents, including translation of High School Diploma Translation, birth certificate, diploma, and more. Government authorities need precise translations to read and understand everything contained in the documents.

Professional Diploma Translation Services

The Diploma is an official document signed by a regulatory authority stating the marriage between two people to be valid. It is one of the most important documents to be translated while migrating into the US from a foreign country, or making the marriage in a foreign country official in the US.

Being an important document of evidence, there is a certain standard set by authorities for receiving the required information. This is why certified translations are required by the government authorities.

Certified Diploma translation Services refers to an additional document accompanying the translated document testifying that the translator is proficient to carry out accurate and complete translations. Professionally certified translators who are language experts in both source and target language as well as experienced in certified translations must be hired to conduct accurate and high-quality translations. Reputed translation agencies with a team of experts are mostly hired by people looking for certified Diploma translations.

USCIS Diploma Translation Services

The USCIS is a federal agency that is responsible for the lawful immigration of foreigners into the US. It requires certified translations of all non-English documents including Diplomas, birth certificates, passports etc.

Certified Diploma translations for the USCIS refer to the verification of the translated document to be accurate and complete as per the translator's ability. It must also contain the translator's name, address, signature, date, and time of certification. In cases where the translation is done by the company, the translated document must contain the stamp and seal of the company, testifying accuracy and completeness.

Occasionally, the documents may require notarization. It needs a public notary to witness the signing of the certified document while verifying the identity of the translator.

To avoid errors in the submission of translated documents to the USCIS, it is advisable to hire the services of professional translation companies that guarantee satisfactory services conducted by translators who are native linguistic experts in the source and target language and have a rich experience in translating important immigration documents like a Diploma.

Benefits of Outsourcing Diploma Translation

More often than not, people resort to advance software translation tools or bilingual candidates for online Diploma translation for USCIS. Well, as the US is strict about the data they receive, inappropriate translations and any kind of uncertainties in the authenticity of the certified translation could lead to a pause in the process by the USCIs. The USCIS issues an RFE (Request for evidence) along with a month to prove authenticity. In cases where further proof is not provided, the application shall be rejected. It is, therefore, advisable to hire professional and experienced Diploma translation services that guarantee accuracy and completeness. Such documents are rarely rejected by the USCIS.

It is important to understand that the USCIS is a significantly important authority in the US. Appropriate regulations must be followed for the translation of immigration documents. Software tools and bilingual candidates can undoubtedly translate word-by-word in the document, but fail in maintaining the contextual integrity and format of the original document.

So, hiring professional services is the key to success.

Items.Title: 在共享老化操作系统上安装 Emacs 28.1 | Installing Emacs 28.1 on a shared aging OS
Items.Description:
我想在不能修改 /usr 的老化操作系统上使用 Emacs 28.1。 我尝试了 Docker，但我也想使用本地命令。 因此，我将 Emacs 从源 tarball 安装到我的主目录。

但是，Emacs 无法验证 TLS 证书。 所以我安装了 GNUTLS、Nettle、Idn 和 Unistring。

 #!/bin/bash export PKG_CONFIG_PATH = $HOME /lib64/pkgconfig: $HOME /lib/pkgconfig export LD_RUN_PATH = $HOME /lib: $HOME /lib64 export LD_LIBRARY_PATH = $HOME /lib: $HOME /lib64 export LDFLAGS = "-L $HOME /lib -L $HOME /lib64" rm -rf libunistring-1.0 libidn2-2.3.2 nettle-3.6 gmp-6.2.1 emacs-28.1 curl https://ftp.gnu.org/gnu/libunistring/libunistring-1.0.tar.gz | tar -xzvf - && \ pushd libunistring-1.0 && \ ./configure --prefix = $HOME && \ make -j ` nproc ` && \ make install && \ popd || exit 1 curl https://ftp.gnu.org/gnu/libidn/libidn2-2.3.2.tar.gz | tar -xzvf - && \ pushd libidn2-2.3.2 && \ ./configure --prefix = $HOME && \ make -j ` nproc ` && \ make install && \ popd || exit 1 curl https://ftp.gnu.org/gnu/nettle/nettle-3.6.tar.gz | tar xzvf - && \ pushd nettle-3.6 && \ ./configure --prefix = $HOME --enable-mini-gmp && \ make -j ` nproc ` && \ make install && \ popd || exit 1 curl https://www.gnupg.org/ftp/gcrypt/gnutls/v3.7/gnutls-3.7.6.tar.xz | tar xJvf - && \ pushd gnutls-3.7.6 && \ ./configure --prefix = $HOME && \ make -j ` nproc ` && \ make install && \ cd .. || exit 1 curl http://ftp.gnu.org/pub/gnu/emacs/emacs-28.1.tar.gz | tar -xzvf - && pushd emacs-28.1 && \ ./configure --prefix = $HOME --with-x-toolkit = no --with-xpm = no --with-jpeg = no --with-jpeg = no --with-gif = no --with-tiff = no --with-png = no && \ make -j ` nproc ` && \ make install && \ cd .. || exit 1 echo echo echo "Emacs 28.1 must be ready!" echo


原文:

I want to use Emacs 28.1 on aging OS that I mustn't modify /usr. I tried Docker but I want to use local command too. SBCL didn't work properly on old Docker. So I install Emacs from a source tarball to my home directory.

However, Emacs failed to verify TLS certs. So I installed GNUTLS, Nettle, Idn, and Unistring.

#!/bin/bash export PKG_CONFIG_PATH=$HOME/lib64/pkgconfig:$HOME/lib/pkgconfig
export LD_RUN_PATH=$HOME/lib:$HOME/lib64
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$HOME/lib:$HOME/lib64
export LDFLAGS="-L$HOME/lib -L$HOME/lib64" rm -rf libunistring-1.0 libidn2-2.3.2 nettle-3.6 gmp-6.2.1 emacs-28.1 curl https://ftp.gnu.org/gnu/libunistring/libunistring-1.0.tar.gz | tar -xzvf - && \ pushd libunistring-1.0 && \ ./configure --prefix=$HOME && \ make -j `nproc` && \ make install && \ popd || exit 1 curl https://ftp.gnu.org/gnu/libidn/libidn2-2.3.2.tar.gz | tar -xzvf - && \ pushd libidn2-2.3.2 && \ ./configure --prefix=$HOME && \ make -j `nproc` && \ make install && \ popd || exit 1 curl https://ftp.gnu.org/gnu/nettle/nettle-3.6.tar.gz | tar xzvf - && \ pushd nettle-3.6 && \ ./configure --prefix=$HOME --enable-mini-gmp && \ make -j `nproc` && \ make install && \ popd || exit 1 curl https://www.gnupg.org/ftp/gcrypt/gnutls/v3.7/gnutls-3.7.6.tar.xz | tar xJvf - && \ pushd gnutls-3.7.6 && \ ./configure --prefix=$HOME && \ make -j `nproc` && \ make install && \ cd .. || exit 1 curl http://ftp.gnu.org/pub/gnu/emacs/emacs-28.1.tar.gz | tar -xzvf - && pushd emacs-28.1 && \ ./configure --prefix=$HOME --with-x-toolkit=no --with-xpm=no --with-jpeg=no --with-jpeg=no --with-gif=no --with-tiff=no --with-png=no && \ make -j `nproc` && \ make install && \ cd .. || exit 1 echo
echo
echo "Emacs 28.1 must be ready!"
echo

Items.Title: 命名空间即服务 | Namespace as a Service
Items.Description:
阿肖克·纳加拉吉

发表于命名空间即服务

  • 集群即服务
  • 控制平面即服务

    • 命名空间是 Kubernetes 中软隔离的手段

    在 Kubernetes 中，命名空间提供了一种在单个集群中隔离资源组的机制。 资源名称在命名空间内必须是唯一的，但跨命名空间不需要。 基于命名空间的作用域仅适用于命名空间对象（例如部署、服务等），不适用于集群范围的对象（例如 StorageClass、Nodes、PersistentVolumes 等）。
    来自 kubernetes.io/docs

    在本系列中，我们将探索多种解决方案“命名空间即服务”（用于 kubernetes 集群内的多租户）。 为简单起见，我们将不讨论 RBAC（身份验证和授权）。

    竞争者

    • 命名空间即服务（使用 vanilla kubernetes 对象）
    • 分层命名空间
    • 来自 Loft 实验室的 vCluster
    • 阁楼实验室的售货亭
    • Capsule from Calstix Calstix 胶囊

    Jan Schenk (he/him) - Jan Schenk (他/他) - May 23 5月23日



    原文:

    Ashok Nagaraj

    Posted on Jun 2

    Kubernetes documentation describes 3 models of multitenancy:

    • namespace as a service
    • cluster as a service
    • control-plane as a service

    Namespaces are the means of soft isolation in kubernetes

    In Kubernetes, namespaces provides a mechanism for isolating groups of resources within a single cluster. Names of resources need to be unique within a namespace, but not across namespaces. Namespace-based scoping is applicable only for namespaced objects (e.g. Deployments, Services, etc) and not for cluster-wide objects (e.g. StorageClass, Nodes, PersistentVolumes, etc).
    From kubernetes.io/docs

    Kubernetes clusters generally being aggregations of computational blocks it makes sense to make them multi-tenant from an economic and efficient use of underlying infrastructure perspective.

    In this series we will explore multiple solutions "Namespace as a Service" (for multitenancy within a kubernetes cluster). For the sake of simplicity we will keep RBAC (authentication and authorization) out of the discussion.

    Contenders

    • Namespace as a service (using vanilla kubernetes objects)
    • Hierarchical namespaces
    • vCluster from Loft labs
    • Kiosk from Loft labs
    • Capsule from Calstix

    Jan Schenk (he/him) - May 23

Items.Title: Azure 逻辑应用的增强监控 | Enhanced monitoring for your Azure Logic App
Items.Description:
实施业务流程可能具有挑战性，因为您通常需要使各种服务协同工作。 想想贵公司用来存储和处理数据的一切。 您如何集成所有这些产品？ 您使用图形设计工具以您需要的任意组合将各个部分组合在一起，逻辑应用程序将在云中自动运行您的流程。 在 Azure 中构建逻辑应用流很简单，但对如此强大的资源的增强监控是强制性的，但在 Azure 中缺乏。 在这篇博客中，让我们讨论监控逻辑应用程序的需求以及 Serverless360 如何提供它。

需要逻辑应用监控

逻辑应用程序是集成业务场景的礼物，因为它们很容易理解并与其他系统集成。 由于它们是不可或缺的一部分，因此监控 Logic App 至关重要。 以下是组织应监控 Logic App 的几个原因，

  • 缺乏Azure Logic Apps 监控可能会影响架构的完整流程，从而导致业务风险。

  • 监控对于避免 Logic App 运行失败的风暴起着至关重要的作用

  • 监视是在实施任何 Azure 资源时要检查的基本参数。

尽管 Azure 具有基于指标的监控，但运营/支持/业务用户在没有整体视图的情况下很难了解应用程序的性能。 如果我们试图了解应用程序级别的性能，我们必须进入各种订阅并深入资源组以手动查找每个资源状态。 它消耗大量时间并导致开销。

让我们看看 Azure 中的挑战，

  1. 识别重新提交的运行。
  2. 仅关注失败的逻辑应用运行。
  3. 若要安排，仅运行所需的失败逻辑应用的自动化。
  4. 修改触发逻辑应用运行的输入消息。
  5. 监控和识别逻辑应用程序中出现的问题。

执行上述任务并不容易，因此用户在 Azure 门户中使用逻辑应用时可能会发现它具有挑战性。 莱姆斯在一个屋檐下。

使用 Serverless360 管理和监控 Azure 逻辑应用

原生 Azure Monitor 帮助其用户做出反应，但任何现实世界的业务都需要主动方法。 为了简要介绍这一点，让我们考虑一个场景，其中队列充当消息传递桥梁，Function 应用程序验证从队列接收到的消息。 由于我们在 peek-lock 模式下检索消息，因此当验证失败时，消息会停滞在同一个队列中。 超过 TTL（生存时间），消息被推送到 DDL（死信队列）。 在我们的场景中，TTL 配置为 1 分钟。

现在考虑订单没有通过验证，DDL消息堆积在队列中。 如果我们使用 Azure 监视器，我们只能在超出最大阈值时收到警报。 但是当我们到达警报时，我们的队列中会堆积更多的 DDL 消息。 如果我们在达到阈值之前有一个主动警报，这将对我们有所帮助。

为了解决这一挑战，用户必须继续使用 Serverless360。 借助 Serverless360，用户可以高效地管理和监控 Azure Logic Apps，解决上述挑战。 让我们看看如何！

识别重新提交的运行。

可以在 Azure 门户中重新提交逻辑应用运行，但在识别重新提交的运行方面的挑战尚不清楚。

单击它将获取实际父运行的详细信息，通过该详细信息可以实现父运行和子运行之间的关联。

仅关注失败的逻辑应用运行。

在 Azure 门户中，用户可能会发现很难了解逻辑应用的哪些实例需要您执行任何操作。 也很难判断使用哪些逻辑应用实例已经解决了问题。

通过使用 Serverless360 中的 Action Required 功能可以轻松解决这一挑战，该功能可以在“Action Required”选项卡中对需要用户注意的运行进行分组。 可以对本节中可用的运行执行诸如重新提交之类的操作。

安排失败的逻辑应用运行的自定义自动化。

用户面临的另一大挑战是仅自动重新提交所需的失败逻辑应用运行。

它可以通过使用 Serverless360 来解决，其中可以自定义失败的运行，以根据过滤器仅重新提交所需的失败逻辑应用运行。

更深入的见解

它提供了一个带有完整应用程序视图的干净仪表板，并根据其监控配置显示每个实体的状态。

增强的逻辑应用监控

它提供具有阈值的指标监控，以使最终用户受益。 借助此监控，您可以维护积极主动的业务环境。

始终有必要及时了解资源的状态。 但是，对于任何企业来说，使用 Serverless360 的规则评估频率中提供的自动更正选项将更加有益。 与所选业务应用程序关联的所有资源都将根据频率进行监控。

根据指定的聚合周期，与所选业务应用程序关联的所有指标都将使用指标的主要聚合类型进行聚合。

了解 Azure 资源的状态对于确保托管在云平台上的业务顺畅流动至关重要。 将在指定时间内发送与该业务应用程序相关且已选择监控的资源的状态报告。 收件人电子邮件配置：警报通常发送到提及的电子邮件地址。 用户可以配置多个电子邮件地址，并选择是一次性将警报发送到所有电子邮件地址还是作为单独的电子邮件发送。

通知通道：除了电子邮件警报，第三方通知通道也可以配置为接收来自 Serverless360 的警报。 可以在显示的列表中选择已在“设置”部分配置的通知渠道。

结论

每个企业都需要加强监控以避免任何停机或故障。 尤其是任何在云端运行的业务，都少不了一个像Serverless360这样的工具。 利用它，可以更有效地监控 Azure 云空间中的业务应用程序。 在 Serverless360 的帮助下，加强您的Azure 逻辑应用监控并获得强大的工具集和可操作的见解来解决问题。



原文:

Implementing a business process can be challenging because you typically need to make various services work together. Think about everything your company uses to store and process data. How do you integrate all these products? Azure Logic Apps gives you pre-built components to connect to hundreds of services. You use a graphical design tool to put the pieces together in any combination you need, and Logic Apps will run your process automatically in the cloud. Building a Logic App flow in Azure is simple, but enhanced monitoring for such a powerful resource is mandatory but lacks in Azure. In this blog, let us discuss the need to monitor the Logic App and how Serverless360 provides it.

Need for Logic App Monitoring

Logic Apps are a gift for integrating business scenarios as they are pretty easy to understand and integrate with other systems. As they play an integral part monitoring the Logic App is vital. Here are a few reasons why organizations should monitor Logic App,

  • Lack of Azure Logic Apps monitoring might affect the complete flow of the architecture, resulting in business risk.

  • Monitoring plays a vital role to avoid the storm of failed Logic App runs

  • Monitoring is an essential parameter to check while implementing any Azure resources.

Challenges in Azure

Though Azure has metric-based monitoring, it is difficult for an Operations/ Support/ Business user to understand the application’s performance without a holistic view. If we try to understand the performance at the application level, we must move into various subscriptions and drill deeper into the resource group to find each resource status manually. It consumes a lot of time and leads to overhead.

Azure portal is powerful to build enterprise-grade solutions but complex to manage and monitor; let us see the challenges in Azure,

  1. To identify the resubmitted runs.
  2. To focus only on the failed Logic App runs.
  3. To schedule, the automation of only the required failed logic app runs.
  4. To modify the input message which triggered the Logic App to run.
  5. To Monitor and identify the issues arising in Logic Apps.

It isn’t easy to perform the above tasks, and hence users might find it challenging when working with Logic Apps in the Azure portal. Serverless360 will be a unified solution to resolve all the prob
lems under one roof.

Manage and Monitor Azure Logic Apps with Serverless360

The native Azure Monitor helps its users be reactive, but any real-world business requires a proactive approach. To brief on this, let us consider a scenario where the queue acts as a messaging bridge, and the Function app validates the messages received from the queue. Since we are retrieving the messages in peek-lock mode, when the validation fails, messages get stagnated in the same queue. Beyond the TTL (Time To Live), the message gets pushed to the DDL(Dead-Lettered Queue). In our scenario, TTL is configured for 1min.

Now consider that the orders fail to pass the validation, and DDL messages are piled up in the queue. If we use the Azure monitor, we can get the alert only when the maximum threshold value is breached. But by the time we reach the alert, there will be lot more DDL messages piled up in our queue. It would be helpful for us if we had a proactive alert before reaching the threshold value.

Azure Logic Apps solve enormous business challenges with easy workflow design and automation but managing and monitoring the Azure Logic Apps in an application context is impossible with Azure Portal. To solve this challenge, the user must go ahead with Serverless360. Serverless360 is the solution for managing and monitoring Azure Serverless Applications. With the help of Serverless360, users can manage and monitor the Azure Logic Apps efficiently, resolving the challenges mentioned above. Let’s see how!

To identify the resubmitted runs.

Logic Apps run resubmission is possible in the Azure portal, but the challenge is unclear in identifying the resubmitted runs.

Serverless360 overcomes this challenge by adding a ‘Resubmission of’ tag to those runs resubmitted either from Serverless360 or from Azure Portal. Clicking it will fetch the details of the actual parent-run through which correlation between parent-run and child-run can be achieved.

To focus only on the failed Logic App runs.

In the Azure portal, users might find it challenging to understand which instances of the Logic App need you to take any action. It will also be tough to tell using which Logic App instances you have already resolved an issue.

This challenge can be easily solved by using the Action Required feature in Serverless360, which can group the runs that require user attention in the ‘Action Required’ tab. Operations like Resubmit can be performed on the runs available in this section.

To schedule the custom automation of failed Logic App runs.

Another big challenge the user faces is to automate the resubmission of only the required failed Logic App runs.

It can be solved by using Serverless360, where the failed runs can be customized to resubmit only required failed Logic App runs based on the filters.

Deeper Insights

Service Map in Serverless360 serves as a physical representation of the architecture, and the user can derive relationships between the entities that constitute the Business application. It provides a clean dashboard with a complete application view and displays the state of each entity based on its monitoring configuration.

Enhanced Monitoring for Logic Apps

Serverless360’s unique monitoring capabilities keep the end-user informed about the performance and status of their business application. It provides metrics monitoring with threshold values to benefit end-users. With the help of this monitoring, you can maintain a proactive business environment.

It is always necessary to stay up to date on the status of resources. However, it will be even more beneficial for any business to use an auto-correct option available in Serverless360’s Rules evaluation frequency. All resources associated with the selected Business Application will be monitored based on the frequency.

The Aggregation period in Serverless360 allows the user to configure a warning threshold value for the metrics of the Resources, which will send us an alert if the configured threshold value is exceeded. Based on the Aggregation Period specified, all metrics associated with the selected Business application will be aggregated using the metric’s Primary Aggregation Type.

Knowing the status of your Azure resources is critical for ensuring the smooth flow of business hosted on a cloud platform. A status report of resources associated with this Business application that has been chosen to be monitored will be sent during the specified hours. The report generated by Serverless360 will be a consolidated monitoring report in which the application level of visibility is achieved.

Recipient email configuration: Alerts are generally sent to mentioned Email addresses. Users can configure more than one email address and choose if the alerts can be sent to all the email addresses in a single go or as separate emails.

Notification channels: Besides Email alerts, third-party Notification Channels can also be configured to receive alerts from Serverless360. Notification Channels already configured in the Settings section can be chosen in the list shown.

Conclusion

Every business needs enhanced monitoring to avoid any downtime or failure. Especially for any business running in the cloud, there is an indispensable need for a tool like Serverless360. Utilizing it, monitoring the Business Application in Azure Cloud Space can be more effective. Serverless360 does not stop with monitoring but also provides better Management solutions for Operations and Support people to enhancing their day in and day out tasks.

Strengthen your Azure Logic Apps monitoring and get powerful toolsets, actionable insights to troubleshoot issues with the help of Serverless360.

Python có các kiểu dữ liệu mặc định như sau: -Text type: str -Numeric Types: int, float, complex -Sequence Types: list, tuple, range -Mapping Type: dict -Set Types: set, frozenset -Boolean Type: bool -Binary Types: bytes, bytearray, memoryview Ngoài ra có các dạng python collections(Arrays): -List: là collection cho phép sắp xếp, có thể thay đổi và trùng lặp phần tử. -Tuple: là collection cho phép sắp xếp và trùng lặp phần tử nhưng không thể thay đổi. -Set: là collection không thể sắp xếp và không trùng lặp phần tử.
